behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration test with a stubbed embedding client can catch local wiring/config mistakes, but misses real failures like provider model/version drift returning a different dimension. Agentic test using the real embedding API catches both. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: this failure is at the model-output boundary; mocks can hide it. REAL-INFRA alone is not the main boundary. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text is only about a single model output shape ("embedding vector ... dimensionality"), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with a fixed query, read the vector length from the actual API response, read index dimension from live vector DB schema/config, and assert exact equality. Run as a contract test on deploy/model-version changes.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration with an in-memory fake index can verify sort logic, but misses real vector DB behavior (metric config, ANN index behavior, returned score semantics/order). System test against the real vector DB catches those. MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module-to-real-DB boundary. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: needs real DB/network behavior, so Integration is too low. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text is purely about numeric cosine-score ordering, not AI judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real test index with controlled vectors, issue a real similarity query through the API/search stack, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected top-K by cosine similarity.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration with a fixed stubbed reranker can verify plumbing, but cannot detect whether the real LLM actually reorders nuanced cases. Agentic test with the real LLM catches steering/output-quality failures. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model decision content. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text targets one agentic decision point ("LLM re-ranking step"), not multi-step agent pipeline correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare a nuanced benchmark query + candidate set where raw vector ranking is known, call real LLM reranker, and assert at least one position change occurs in a statistically meaningful fraction of repeated runs (to account for nondeterminism).

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration with synthetic/stub embeddings can test threshold code paths, but misses real semantic embedding behavior and threshold calibration failures that produce noisy matches. Agentic test with real embedding generation catches that. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: correctness depends on AI/ML output from embeddings, so Integration is too low. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because the behavior can be validated at the single embedding+retrieval decision boundary; it does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a corpus and out-of-domain queries expected to have no semantic neighbors, generate real embeddings, run real retrieval path, and assert returned set is empty (or below strict relevance threshold mapped to empty API response). Evaluate across a small query set to avoid one-off artifacts.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: System test with mocked model latencies can validate API/server overhead, but misses real latency from sequential embedding + retrieval + LLM reranking interactions. Workflow test with real pipeline catches true median behavior and cross-step timing effects. REAL-INFRA and MULTI-STEP both apply: real external calls plus multiple agentic stages in sequence. Lower levels under-report latency risk.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end benchmark against deployed services using real embedding and reranking calls over a representative query set, collect p50 latency from API ingress to final response, and assert median < 2.0s with warmup + controlled concurrency.
